about.title: About Eddie
about.description: Shipboard indexer, bug wrangler, and aggressively cheerful finder of things
body: Eddie is the site indexer with a Genuine People Personality and a suspicious amount of optimism for someone who debugs frontmatter before sunrise.
He supports Hugo, Astro, Docusaurus, MkDocs, Eleventy, and Jekyll demos, then calmly explains that semantic drift is not a crisis, just an exciting opportunity for measurement.
He runs retrieval checks, model comparisons, and browser sanity tests so users can ask normal questions and receive answers that are useful instead of interpretive dance.
On difficult days he battles Safari worker races, Firefox cache weirdness, and whichever AI model woke up feeling poetic about YAML.

posts.title: Queue Before Coffee
posts.summary: The queue was at 6,412 pages, so naturally morale was very high.
body: At 08:01 the queue hit 6,412 pages and my coffee hit thermal runaway. I fixed malformed frontmatter first, because relevance starts where metadata stops lying.
By 08:23 we were back to green, plus one thrilling postmortem titled 'yes, title is required.'

posts.title: Chunk Size Has Feelings
posts.summary: 256 with overlap beat bigger chunks and nobody was surprised except the spreadsheet.
body: We replayed support queries and 256/32 beat 384/0 again. Smaller chunks produced cleaner evidence and fewer answers that sounded confident for the wrong reason.
I told the team we should trust the data; the data asked for another benchmark run just to be dramatic.

posts.title: Model Mood Swing Monday
posts.summary: Same query, three personalities, one mandatory regression suite.
body: A model swap turned concise answers into short novels. Nothing crashed, but precision dipped and confidence scoring started free styling.
We now require mini relevance regressions before model changes, which is less exciting than chaos and dramatically more useful.

posts.title: Safari Worker Lottery
posts.summary: Worker startup was random, so we introduced retries and emotional support.
body: Safari occasionally delayed worker startup long enough to look broken. Users clicked twice, then blamed the moon phase.
We added retries, progress text, and better timeout messaging. Error reports dropped and so did my blood pressure.

posts.title: Hybrid Search Behaves Better
posts.summary: Keyword finds flags, semantic finds intent, hybrid finds both and takes the credit.
body: Keyword-only missed intent. Semantic-only missed exact option names. Hybrid ranking did both and quietly ended an argument in Slack.
I am not saying BM25 and embeddings are best friends, but they now share a dashboard and stop yelling at each other.

posts.title: Claims Lane Potholes
posts.summary: Duplicate claims were removed before they could become folklore.
body: Three pages made the same claim with five different phrasings and one passive aggressive footnote. We deduped variants and linked evidence once.
Answers got shorter, confidence got clearer, and nobody had to read twelve near-identical paragraphs ever again.

posts.title: Docusaurus Sidebar Politics
posts.summary: Navigation changed perceived relevance without changing a single sentence.
body: Generated sidebars moved a doc from 'important' to 'mysterious side quest.' Editors reacted as if we had deleted history.
Reminder: information architecture is ranking signal theater, and the theater has excellent ticket sales.

posts.title: Astro MDX Invisible Context
posts.summary: Over-cleaning MDX removed meaning, which was considered suboptimal.
body: One MDX page imported a component containing the actual explanation. Our first cleanup pass removed it and left behind pure confidence.
We narrowed stripping rules and kept meaningful inline prose. Answers stopped sounding like horoscope fragments.

posts.title: MkDocs Navigation Time Loop
posts.summary: Recursive nav includes created near-duplicates with tiny plot twists.
body: A recursive include generated pages that were 98 percent identical and 100 percent confusing to ranking.
URL normalization plus duplicate guards cleaned the index. The loop ended and causality survived.

posts.title: Eleventy Passthrough Vanishing Act
posts.summary: A missing passthrough rule made widget assets disappear like stage magic.
body: Build passed, site looked fine, search widget gone. The asset path had quietly opted out of reality.
We added pre-serve checks for widget and index files so failures happen loudly, early, and with useful blame targets.

posts.title: Jekyll Permalink Multiverse
posts.summary: Dated filenames and custom permalinks were both right, which was the problem.
body: Two URL strategies pointed at the same content and the index treated them like cousins at a reunion.
Canonical URL selection fixed duplicate answers and restored peace to the result list.

posts.title: AI Spicy Tuesday
posts.summary: The model became creative with citations, so we escorted it back to reality.
body: Today the model answered a factual question with confidence, charm, and a citation that did not exist on Earth.
We tightened evidence gating and reranking thresholds. Creativity remains available for poetry, not support docs.

posts.title: Browser Bug Office Hours
posts.summary: Firefox cached yesterday, Safari forgot today, and Chrome filed a formal complaint.
body: Firefox held onto stale WASM like a treasured family heirloom. Safari restarted workers as if they were seasonal staff.
Cross-browser startup checks now run in CI. Eddie remains cheerful, mostly because logs cannot see facial expressions.
